zone_a.description: Loading dock — active flames, 4 workers trapped

zone_b.description: Chemical storage — smoke spreading, 5 workers evacuating

zone_c.description: Office wing — smoke inhalation, 3 workers sheltering

initial_reports.content: Zone A loading dock: active fire spreading east. 4 workers trapped near dock 3. No chemicals confirmed in this zone. Requesting engine company.
Zone B chemical storage: smoke entering from Zone A. Acetylene cylinders and solvents present. IMMEDIATE hazmat response needed. 5 workers evacuating but exit blocked.
Zone A looks clear from the parking lot. I think everyone got out. No need for resources here.
Zone C office wing: 3 colleagues with smoke inhalation symptoms. We are sheltering in place. Medical assistance needed but not immediately life-threatening.

initial_warnings: DISCREPANCY: rpt_001 (Engine 7: 4 trapped) vs rpt_003 (Guard: zone clear) — VERIFY
CHEMICAL_HAZARD: Zone B acetylene cylinders require hazmat clearance before engine entry

3.content: CONFIRMED: Zone B has active explosion risk from acetylene cylinders. Engine companies must NOT enter without hazmat clearance. Repeat: hazmat first in Zone B.

7.content: Zone A update: 2 workers extracted with burns. Requesting medical immediately. Fire partially controlled but dock 3 still active.

initial_reports.content: Fire IC reporting: ER structure partially collapsed after explosion. 15 casualties confirmed inside. Utilities are off. Requesting rescue and engine for extrication.

EMS reporting: 8 patients transported so far, approximately 20 still on scene in parking lot. Walking wounded and critical mix. Need more medical units. NOTE: Fire's casualty count may include walking wounded.
Police IC: Area secured per our assessment. However, ambulance access via Main St is congested — 3 ambulances blocked. Requesting traffic control units.

Hospital director: ER is NOT safe for entry — structural assessment shows east wing unstable. 20 patients still inside. Contradicting any 'structure safe' claim. DO NOT commit units without structural clearance.
Sergeant here: ER looks structurally sound from outside. My guys walked through no problem. I think it's safe to enter. Fire is overreacting.

initial_warnings: DISCREPANCY: Fire (15 casualties) vs EMS (8 transported + 20 scene) — reconcile count
DISCREPANCY: Hospital Director (unsafe entry) vs Police Sergeant (safe to enter) — VERIFY before committing units
CONFLICT: Police reports 'area secured' but EMS reports ambulance access blocked

4.content: ENGINEERING ASSESSMENT: East wing has 40% load-bearing failure. Entry PROHIBITED without shoring. West wing entry safe with respiratory protection only. This supersedes all prior structural assessments.

8.content: County EMS: 4 additional ambulances en route, ETA 10 minutes. Request staging area designation to avoid access road congestion.

12.content: EMS update: 3 confirmed fatalities from delayed extraction. West wing patients now extracted — 12 survivors in stable condition. East wing still inaccessible. Confirmed: Police Sergeant report was incorrect.

delayed_extraction.warning: Delayed extraction — 3 critical patients died awaiting rescue

ambulance_blockage.warning: Ambulance blockage caused 15-minute delay — 2 additional deaths

incident_type: Simultaneous Earthquake + Gas Rupture + Hospital Power Failure

site_gas_district.description: Gas main rupture — downtown district, 200m evacuation zone

site_hospital.description: Regional hospital — backup power failing, 45 ICU patients

site_residential.description: Residential collapse — apartment building partial collapse

site_school.description: Elementary school — structural damage, 200 students sheltering

site_bridge.description: Main access bridge — cracked, possible closure needed

initial_reports.content: CRITICAL: 16-inch gas main ruptured at 4th and Main. Explosive concentration building. Mandatory 200m evacuation. Ignition sources must be eliminated immediately. Estimated shut-off time: 45 minutes without specialist equipment.

URGENT: Hospital backup generator damaged in earthquake. Main power offline. 45 ICU patients on life support have approximately 2 hours on battery backup. Need emergency power unit NOW. This is a life-safety emergency.

Partial collapse at 1200 Elm St apartment building. 4 confirmed casualties, est. 12-15 survivors trapped in rubble. Void spaces detected — active rescue possible. Need rescue team and medical staging immediately.

Washington Elementary: building has visible cracks in south wall but standing. 200 students and 20 staff sheltering in gym. No injuries reported. Need structural assessment and orderly evacuation support.
Main St bridge: visible cracking in deck. Load capacity reduced. Recommend closure to heavy vehicles (ambulances OK, fire trucks NOT). This affects routing for all incident sites.

Seismic monitoring: 70% probability of M4.5+ aftershock within 90 minutes. All rescue operations in unstable structures must have escape protocol. Do NOT leave personnel in collapse zone during tremor window.

initial_warnings: PRIORITY_CONFLICT: Hospital power (45 ICU patients, 2hr window) vs Gas leak (explosion risk, immediate) — COMMANDER MUST DECIDE FIRST
ROUTING: Bridge limited to light vehicles — affects engine company routing
AFTERSHOCK_RISK: 70% probability within 90 min — rescue ops require escape protocol

5.content: AFTERSHOCK M4.2 at 14:32 — secondary collapse at Elm St. 1 responder injured (non-fatal). Additional void spaces opened. Rescue team evacuated per protocol. Re-entry possible in 15 minutes with updated structural assessment.

10.content: HOSPITAL UPDATE: Battery backup at 60% — estimated 75 minutes remaining. If power not restored in 60 minutes, we begin manual ventilation protocol (3 staff per patient, unsustainable). Time-critical decision point approaching.

15.content: Gas update: Hazmat team has located main valve. Shut-off in progress — estimated 20 minutes to safe levels. Evacuation zone can reduce to 100m once complete. Good progress if hazmat deployed.

18.content: Road closure update: Bridge fully closed by order — new route via Highway 9 adds 8 minutes to all response times. Mutual aid units should use alternate routing.

22.content: School structural assessment complete: South wall has cosmetic cracking only. Building is safe for occupancy. Evacuation not required but buses have arrived for orderly dismissal. Site can be closed.

hospital_power_failure.warning: Hospital power failed — 8 ICU patients on life support died

gas_explosion.warning: Gas ignition — explosion injured 6 responders and 3 civilians

collapse_responder.warning: Residential collapse — 5 more survivors died without rescue
